pattern: Sum the proper divisors of a number efficiently by iterating only up to its square root and adding divisor pairs.
hints: A proper divisor is any divisor strictly less than the number itself, including one.
Divisors come in pairs around the square root, so checking i up to the square root finds both i and num divided by i.
Accumulate one for each number, add both members of every divisor pair (avoiding double-counting a perfect square root), then compare the total to the original.

edge_inputs.description: one has no proper divisors summing to itself
perfect square that is not perfect
large non-perfect value near the upper bound

stress.description: random values across the full range, mostly non-perfect
